structures.name: Basic contractions
structures.description: Shortening two words into one with an apostrophe (I'm, it's, isn't, what's), and keeping it's apart from its.

rules.en: A contraction joins two words into one. The apostrophe (') shows where letters are missing.
rules.de: Eine Kurzform verbindet zwei Wörter zu einem. Der Apostroph (') zeigt, wo Buchstaben fehlen.

rules.en: Don't mix up it's (= it is) with its (= belonging to it).
rules.de: Verwechsle nicht it's (= it is) mit its (= sein/ihr, Besitz).

examples.en: It's a nice day. (= It is)
The dog loves its ball. (= possession)

commonErrors.description: Leaving out the apostrophe in a contraction.
Confusing it's (it is) with its (possessive).
commonErrors.wrong: The cat is eating it's food.
commonErrors.correct: The cat is eating its food.

structures.name: Imperatives
structures.description: Telling someone to do or not to do something, using the base form of the verb and Don't for the negative.

rules.en: To tell someone to do something, use the base form of the verb (without to) and no subject.
rules.de: Um jemandem zu sagen, dass er etwas tun soll, verwendest du die Grundform des Verbs (ohne to) und kein Subjekt.

rules.en: To tell someone not to do something, use Don't (Do not) + base form.
rules.de: Um zu sagen, dass jemand etwas nicht tun soll, verwendest du Don't (Do not) + Grundform.

commonErrors.description: Adding to before the verb.
Including the subject you.
Using not instead of don't for the negative.

structures.name: Plural nouns + irregular plurals (1)
structures.description: Forming the plural of nouns: regular -s, the consonant + y → -ies spelling change, and the first irregular plurals (child–children, fish–fish).

rules.en: Most nouns form the plural by adding -s.
rules.de: Den Plural von Nomen bildest du normalerweise, indem du ein -s anhängst.

rules.en: When a noun ends in a consonant + y, the plural ending is -ies. But after a vowel + y, just add -s.
rules.de: Endet ein Nomen auf Konsonant + y, schreibst du die Pluralendung -ies. Steht ein Vokal vor dem -y, hängst du nur -s an.

rules.en: Some nouns are exceptions: they have a special plural form you learn by heart.
rules.de: Manche Nomen sind Ausnahmen: Sie haben eine besondere Pluralform, die du auswendig lernst.

commonErrors.description: Regularising an irregular plural.
commonErrors.wrong: There are three childs in the garden.
commonErrors.correct: There are three children in the garden.

commonErrors.description: Forgetting to change consonant + y to -ies.
Capitalising nouns as in German.

structures.name: Questions (name, email, how are you)
structures.description: Asking for someone's name, email address and how they are, with the matching short answers.

rules.en: Ask for a name or email address with What's …?; ask someone to spell it with Can you spell it, please?
rules.de: Nach Namen oder E-Mail-Adresse fragst du mit What's …?; um das Buchstabieren bittest du mit Can you spell it, please?

examples.de: Wie heißt du? – Ich bin Sue.

examples.en: What's your email address? Can you spell it, please?
examples.de: Wie ist deine E-Mail-Adresse? Kannst du sie bitte buchstabieren?

rules.en: Ask how someone is with How are you? and answer with I'm fine, thanks. And you?
rules.de: Wie es jemandem geht, fragst du mit How are you?; du antwortest mit I'm fine, thanks. And you?

examples.en: How are you? – I'm fine, thanks. And you?
examples.de: Wie geht es dir? – Mir geht es gut, danke. Und dir?

commonErrors.description: Using the wrong question word for a name.
Answering How are you? with an age or name.
commonErrors.correct: How are you? – I'm fine, thanks.

structures.name: Prepositions of place
structures.description: Saying where something is: in, on, under, behind, next to, in front of.

rules.en: Use in (inside), on (on a surface), under (below), behind (at the back), next to (beside) and in front of (facing).
rules.de: Verwende in (drinnen), on (auf einer Oberfläche), under (darunter), behind (dahinter), next to (daneben) und in front of (davor).

examples.en: Where's the frog? – It's in the shoe.
examples.de: Wo ist der Frosch? – Er ist im Schuh.

examples.en: The cat is under the bed.
examples.de: Die Katze ist unter dem Bett.

examples.en: The car is in front of the house.
examples.de: Das Auto ist vor dem Haus.

commonErrors.description: Forgetting of in in front of.
commonErrors.wrong: The tree is in front the house.
commonErrors.correct: The tree is in front of the house.

commonErrors.description: Using in instead of at for a location.

structures.name: Subject pronouns
structures.description: The personal pronouns used as the subject of a sentence: I, you, he, she, it, we, they.

rules.en: Subject pronouns replace a noun as the subject: I, you, he, she, it, we, they.
rules.de: Subjektpronomen ersetzen ein Nomen als Subjekt: I, you, he, she, it, we, they.

examples.en: Tom is my friend. He is twelve.
examples.de: Tom ist mein Freund. Er ist zwölf.

examples.en: The book is new. It is interesting.
examples.de: Das Buch ist neu. Es ist interessant.

rules.en: Use it for things and for animals (when you don't know the gender), never for people.
rules.de: Verwende it für Dinge und für Tiere (wenn du das Geschlecht nicht kennst), nie für Menschen.

examples.en: Where is the dog? – It is in the garden.
examples.de: Wo ist der Hund? – Er ist im Garten.

examples.en: This is my sister. She is nice.
examples.de: Das ist meine Schwester. Sie ist nett.

commonErrors.description: Using he or she for an object.
commonErrors.wrong: The table is big. He is brown.
commonErrors.correct: The table is big. It is brown.

commonErrors.description: Using it for a person.
commonErrors.wrong: This is my teacher. It is very nice.
commonErrors.correct: This is my teacher. She is very nice.

structures.name: there is / there are
structures.nameDe: there is / there are – es gibt
structures.description: Saying that something exists: there is for singular, there are for plural.

rules.en: Use there is (there's) for one thing (singular).
rules.de: Verwende there is (there's) für eine Sache (Einzahl).

examples.en: There's a parrot in the tree.
examples.de: Es gibt einen Papagei im Baum.

rules.en: Use there are for more than one thing (plural).
rules.de: Verwende there are für mehrere Sachen (Mehrzahl).

examples.en: There are three frogs on the desk.
examples.de: Es gibt drei Frösche auf dem Tisch.

commonErrors.description: Using there is with a plural noun.
commonErrors.wrong: There is three monkeys in the zoo.
commonErrors.correct: There are three monkeys in the zoo.

commonErrors.description: Using it has instead of there is.
commonErrors.wrong: It has a park in our town.
commonErrors.correct: There is a park in our town.

structures.name: to be (affirmative)
structures.description: The affirmative forms of the verb be (am/is/are) in long and short form.

rules.en: The verb be has the forms I am, you are, he/she/it is, we/you/they are. You can write the long form (I am) or the short form (I'm).
rules.de: Das Verb be hat die Formen I am, you are, he/she/it is, we/you/they are. Du kannst die Langform (I am) oder die Kurzform (I'm) schreiben.

examples.en: He's in class 4A. (He is in class 4A.)
They're from London. (They are from London.)

commonErrors.description: Leaving out the verb be (copula).
Using the wrong form of be for the subject.

structures.name: have got / haven't got
structures.description: Expressing possession with have got in affirmative, negative and question forms (has got for he/she/it).

rules.en: Use have got to say you possess something. For he/she/it the form is has got.
rules.de: Mit have got sagst du, dass du etwas hast. Für he/she/it lautet die Form has got.

examples.en: I have got a cat. / I've got a cat.
He has got a small nose. / He's got a small nose.

rules.en: The negative is haven't got / hasn't got.
rules.de: Die Verneinung lautet haven't got / hasn't got.

rules.en: Questions: Have you got …? / Has she got …? Short answers: Yes, I have. / No, she hasn't.
rules.de: Fragen: Have you got …? / Has she got …? Kurzantworten: Yes, I have. / No, she hasn't.

examples.en: Have you got a cat? – Yes, I have.
Has he got a dog? – No, he hasn't.

commonErrors.description: Using have instead of has with he/she/it.
commonErrors.wrong: He have got a new bike.
commonErrors.correct: He has got a new bike.

commonErrors.description: Using do/does for have got questions.
commonErrors.wrong: Does he has got a cat?

structures.name: Irregular plurals (2)
structures.description: More irregular plurals with a vowel change: foot–feet, tooth–teeth. Recurs from the unit-1 plurals box.

rules.en: Some nouns make the plural by changing a vowel, not by adding -s. Learn them by heart.
rules.de: Manche Nomen bilden den Plural durch einen Vokalwechsel, nicht mit -s. Lerne sie auswendig.

commonErrors.description: Regularising a vowel-change plural.
Regularising tooth.

structures.name: to be (negative) + adjectives
structures.nameDe: to be – sein (verneinte Form) mit Adjektiven
structures.description: The negative of be (am not / isn't / aren't), typically with an adjective after it.

rules.en: Make the negative of be with am not / isn't (is not) / aren't (are not). Note: I'm not (there is no amn't).
rules.de: Die Verneinung von be bildest du mit am not / isn't (is not) / aren't (are not). Beachte: I'm not (es gibt kein amn't).

rules.en: An adjective comes after be and never changes for singular, plural or gender.
rules.de: Ein Adjektiv steht nach be und verändert sich nie für Einzahl, Mehrzahl oder Geschlecht.

examples.en: The boy is tall. The girls are tall.
examples.de: Der Bub ist groß. Die Mädchen sind groß.

commonErrors.description: Putting very before be instead of before the adjective.
Leaving out be before an adjective.

structures.name: Questions with to be
structures.description: Forming yes/no questions and short answers with the verb be by swapping subject and verb.

rules.en: Make questions with be by swapping the subject and be: Am I …? / Is he …? / Are they …? Short answers: Yes, I am. / No, he isn't.
rules.de: Fragen mit be bildest du, indem du Subjekt und be tauschst: Am I …? / Is he …? / Are they …? Kurzantworten: Yes, I am. / No, he isn't.

examples.en: Are you happy? – Yes, I am.
Is she happy? – No, she isn't.

commonErrors.description: Adding do to make a be question.
Using a contraction in an affirmative short answer.

structures.name: can / can't
structures.description: Expressing ability with the modal verb can / can't + base verb (same for all persons).

rules.en: Use can + base verb to say someone is able to do something. can is the same for all persons (no -s for he/she/it) and is always used with a main verb.
rules.de: Mit can + Grundform sagst du, dass jemand etwas kann. can ist für alle Personen gleich (kein -s bei he/she/it) und steht immer mit einem Vollverb.

rules.en: The negative is can't (cannot) + base verb.
rules.de: Die Verneinung lautet can't (cannot) + Grundform.

commonErrors.description: Adding -s to can for he/she/it.
Adding to after can.
Using don't can instead of can't.

sbRefs: g1/sb/SB Unit 5- This is our band.txt#grammar-1

provenance.sbRef: g1/sb/SB Unit 5- This is our band.txt#grammar-1

structures.name: Possessives (possessive adjectives)
structures.description: Words that go before a noun to show who something belongs to: my, your, his, her, its, our, their.

rules.en: Possessives go before a noun and show who something belongs to: my, your, his, her, its, our, their.
rules.de: Possessivbegleiter stehen vor dem Nomen und zeigen, wem etwas gehört: my, your, his, her, its, our, their.

rules.en: its (no apostrophe) = belonging to it; it's (with apostrophe) = it is.
rules.de: its (ohne Apostroph) = sein/ihr (Besitz); it's (mit Apostroph) = it is.

examples.en: This elephant can wiggle its ears.
examples.de: Dieser Elefant kann mit seinen Ohren wackeln.

commonErrors.description: Using a subject pronoun instead of a possessive.
Writing it's when you mean the possessive its.

sbRefs: g1/sb/SB Unit 5- This is our band.txt#grammar-1

provenance.sbRef: g1/sb/SB Unit 5- This is our band.txt#grammar-1

structures.name: a lot of / lots of
structures.nameDe: a lot of / lots of – viel(e)
structures.description: Expressing a large quantity with a lot of or lots of, used with countable and uncountable nouns.

rules.en: a lot of and lots of both mean many/much and work with countable and uncountable nouns.
rules.de: a lot of und lots of bedeuten beide viel/viele und passen zu zählbaren und unzählbaren Nomen.

examples.en: a lot of homework / lots of homework
a lot of books / lots of books

commonErrors.description: Writing a lots of instead of a lot of.
commonErrors.wrong: I have a lots of books.
commonErrors.correct: I have a lot of books.

commonErrors.description: Leaving out of before the noun.
commonErrors.correct: She has a lot of friends.

sbRefs: g1/sb/SB Unit 6- The world's best detective.txt#grammar-1

provenance.sbRef: g1/sb/SB Unit 6- The world's best detective.txt#grammar-1

structures.name: Present simple (affirmative)
structures.description: Talking about habits, likes and facts. Base form for I/you/we/they; add -s for he/she/it.

rules.en: Use the present simple for habits, likes and general facts. I/you/we/they use the base form of the verb.
rules.de: Das Present simple verwendest du für Gewohnheiten, Vorlieben und allgemeine Tatsachen. I/you/we/they verwenden die Grundform des Verbs.

rules.en: For he/she/it add -s to the verb.
rules.de: Bei he/she/it hängst du ein -s an das Verb.

rules.en: Spelling for he/she/it: verbs ending in -o, -ch, -sh, -s, -x add -es; consonant + y changes to -ies.
rules.de: Schreibweise bei he/she/it: Verben auf -o, -ch, -sh, -s, -x bekommen -es; Konsonant + y wird zu -ies.

commonErrors.description: Forgetting the -s for he/she/it (the #1 learner error).
Wrong spelling of a third-person form.

sbRefs: g1/sb/SB Unit 6- The world's best detective.txt#grammar-1

provenance.sbRef: g1/sb/SB Unit 6- The world's best detective.txt#grammar-1

structures.name: Adverbs of frequency
structures.description: Saying how often something happens (always, usually, often, sometimes, never) and where the adverb goes.

rules.en: Frequency adverbs (always, usually, often, sometimes, never) go directly before the main verb.
rules.de: Häufigkeitsadverbien (always, usually, often, sometimes, never) stehen direkt vor dem Hauptverb.

rules.en: With the verb be (am/is/are), the frequency adverb goes after the verb.
rules.de: Beim Verb be (am/is/are) steht das Häufigkeitsadverb nach dem Verb.

commonErrors.description: Putting the adverb after the main verb (German word order).
Putting the adverb before be instead of after it.

structures.name: Articles a / an
structures.description: The indefinite article: a before a consonant sound, an before a vowel sound.

rules.en: Use a before a word that begins with a consonant sound.
rules.de: Verwende a vor einem Wort, das mit einem Konsonantenlaut beginnt.

rules.en: Use an before a word that begins with a vowel sound.
rules.de: Verwende an vor einem Wort, das mit einem Vokallaut beginnt.

commonErrors.description: Using a before a vowel sound.
commonErrors.wrong: I eat a apple every day.
commonErrors.correct: I eat an apple every day.

commonErrors.description: Using an before a consonant sound.

structures.name: Present simple (negative)
structures.description: Forming negatives in the present simple with don't / doesn't + base verb.

rules.en: I/you/we/they + don't (do not) + base verb.
rules.de: I/you/we/they + don't (do not) + Grundform.

rules.en: he/she/it + doesn't (does not) + base verb (no -s on the verb!).
rules.de: he/she/it + doesn't (does not) + Grundform (kein -s am Verb!).

commonErrors.description: Keeping the -s on the verb after doesn't.
Using don't with he/she/it instead of doesn't.

structures.name: Present simple (questions)
structures.description: Forming yes/no questions and short answers in the present simple with Do/Does + subject + base verb.

rules.en: Do + I/you/we/they + base verb? Short answers: Yes, I do. / No, I don't.
rules.de: Do + I/you/we/they + Grundform? Kurzantworten: Yes, I do. / No, I don't.

examples.en: Do you buy your own clothes? – Yes, I do.
examples.de: Kaufst du deine Kleidung selbst? – Ja.

examples.en: Do they wear blue jeans? – No, they don't.

rules.en: Does + he/she/it + base verb? (no -s on the verb!) Short answers: Yes, he does. / No, he doesn't.
rules.de: Does + he/she/it + Grundform? (kein -s am Verb!) Kurzantworten: Yes, he does. / No, he doesn't.

examples.en: Does he like T-shirts with animals? – Yes, he does.
examples.de: Mag er T-Shirts mit Tieren? – Ja.

examples.en: Does she wear yellow trainers? – No, she doesn't.

commonErrors.description: Keeping the -s on the main verb in a does question.
Using do instead of does with he/she/it.

structures.name: Irregular plurals (3)
structures.description: More irregular plurals: mouse–mice and the consonant + y → -ies spelling (pony–ponies). Recurs from the unit-1 plurals box.

rules.en: mouse has the special plural mice. Nouns ending in consonant + y change -y to -ies, e.g. pony – ponies.
rules.de: mouse hat den besonderen Plural mice. Nomen auf Konsonant + y werden zu -ies, z. B. pony – ponies.

commonErrors.description: Regularising mouse.
Forgetting to change -y to -ies in pony.

structures.name: Object pronouns
structures.description: Personal pronouns used as the object of a verb or after a preposition: me, you, him, her, it, us, them.

rules.en: Object pronouns replace a noun as the object: me, you, him, her, it, us, them.
rules.de: Objektpronomen ersetzen ein Nomen als Objekt: me, you, him, her, it, us, them.

commonErrors.description: Using a subject pronoun as the object.
Using a subject pronoun after a preposition.

structures.name: Possessive 's
structures.description: Showing that something belongs to a person by adding 's to a name or noun.

rules.en: Add 's to a name or person word to say something belongs to them: Name + 's + thing.
rules.de: Hänge 's an einen Namen oder eine Personenbezeichnung, um zu sagen, dass etwas dazugehört: Name + 's + Sache.

examples.en: Mr White's pet is a shark.
examples.de: Mr Whites Haustier ist ein Hai.

rules.en: Don't confuse possessive 's with the 's of is. The context tells you which one it is.
rules.de: Verwechsle das Besitz-'s nicht mit dem 's von is. Der Kontext zeigt dir, was gemeint ist.

examples.en: Tom's school bag is big. (belonging to Tom)

commonErrors.description: Leaving out the possessive 's.
Confusing possessive 's with the is contraction.

structures.name: Question words
structures.description: Asking open questions with question words: what, where, how often (and who, why).

rules.en: Use a question word + do/does + subject + base verb to ask for more than a yes/no answer: what (thing), where (place), how often (frequency).
rules.de: Verwende Fragewort + do/does + Subjekt + Grundform, wenn du mehr als ja/nein wissen willst: what (Sache), where (Ort), how often (Häufigkeit).

examples.en: How often do you phone your friends?
examples.de: Wie oft rufst du deine Freunde an?

rules.en: When who is the subject, don't use do/does.
rules.de: Wenn who das Subjekt ist, verwendest du kein do/does.

commonErrors.description: Using the wrong question word.
Leaving out do-support in a Wh-question.

structures.name: How much is / are …?
structures.nameDe: How much is / are …? – Wie viel kostet …?
structures.description: Asking about prices: How much is …? for singular, How much are …? for plural (note jeans = plural).

rules.en: Use How much is …? with a singular noun.
rules.de: Verwende How much is …? mit einem Nomen in der Einzahl.

rules.en: Use How much are …? with a plural noun. Note: jeans = plural.
rules.de: Verwende How much are …? mit einem Nomen in der Mehrzahl. Achtung: jeans = Plural.

examples.en: How much are the green T-shirts?
examples.de: Wie viel kosten die grünen T-Shirts?

commonErrors.description: Using is with a plural noun.
Using costs instead of the be structure.

structures.name: this/that – these/those
structures.description: Pointing words: this/these for things near you, that/those for things further away.

rules.en: Use this (singular) and these (plural) for things near you.
rules.de: Verwende this (Einzahl) und these (Mehrzahl) für Dinge in deiner Nähe.

examples.de: Ich hätte gern dieses T-Shirt, Papa.

rules.en: Use that (singular) and those (plural) for things further away.
rules.de: Verwende that (Einzahl) und those (Mehrzahl) für Dinge, die weiter weg sind.

examples.de: Ich hätte gern diesen roten Pullover (dort).
Ich hätte gern diese blauen Turnschuhe (dort).

commonErrors.description: Using a singular demonstrative with a plural noun.
Using that with a plural noun.

structures.name: Present continuous
structures.description: Describing what is happening right now with am/is/are + verb-ing in all forms.

rules.en: Use the present continuous for actions happening now: subject + am/is/are + verb-ing.
rules.de: Das Present continuous verwendest du für Handlungen, die gerade jetzt passieren: Subjekt + am/is/are + Verb-ing.

rules.en: Negative: subject + am not / isn't / aren't + verb-ing.
rules.de: Verneinung: Subjekt + am not / isn't / aren't + Verb-ing.

examples.de: Ich helfe gerade nicht meiner Mama.

rules.en: Questions: Am/Is/Are + subject + verb-ing? Short answers: Yes, I am. / No, he isn't.
rules.de: Fragen: Am/Is/Are + Subjekt + Verb-ing? Kurzantworten: Yes, I am. / No, he isn't.

examples.en: Are you playing a computer game? – Yes, I am.
examples.de: Spielst du gerade ein Computerspiel? – Ja.

examples.en: Is Peter doing his homework? – No, he isn't.
examples.de: Macht Peter gerade seine Hausübung? – Nein.

commonErrors.description: Leaving out the be auxiliary.
commonErrors.correct: She is playing football right now.

commonErrors.description: Using do-support for present-continuous questions.

structures.name: Ordinal numbers
structures.description: Forming ordinal numbers (first, second, third …): -st/-nd/-rd for 1/2/3, otherwise -th, with spelling exceptions.

rules.en: Most ordinals add -th to the number. Remember: 1 → -st, 2 → -nd, 3 → -rd, otherwise always -th.
rules.de: Die meisten Ordnungszahlen hängen -th an die Zahl. Merke: 1 → -st, 2 → -nd, 3 → -rd, sonst immer -th.

examples.en: one → first, two → second, three → third
examples.de: one → first, two → second, three → third

examples.en: four → fourth, seven → seventh, ten → tenth
examples.de: four → fourth, seven → seventh, ten → tenth

rules.en: Watch the spelling: five → fifth, eight → eighth, nine → ninth, twelve → twelfth. Ordinals above 20 follow the same pattern: twenty-first, thirty-second.
rules.de: Achte auf die Schreibweise: five → fifth, eight → eighth, nine → ninth, twelve → twelfth. Ordnungszahlen über 20 bildest du nach dem gleichen Prinzip: twenty-first, thirty-second.

examples.en: She lives on the fifth floor.
My birthday is on the twenty-third of May.
examples.de: Mein Geburtstag ist am dreiundzwanzigsten Mai.

commonErrors.description: Wrong spelling of an irregular ordinal.
commonErrors.wrong: She was fiveth in the race.
commonErrors.correct: She was fifth in the race.

commonErrors.description: Keeping the -e in ninth.
commonErrors.wrong: His birthday is on the nineth.
commonErrors.correct: His birthday is on the ninth.

structures.name: Past simple (1) was / were
structures.description: The past tense of be: was (I/he/she/it), were (you/we/they), in all forms.

rules.en: Use the past simple to talk about the past. I/he/she/it + was; you/we/they + were.
rules.de: Das Past simple verwendest du, um etwas Vergangenes zu erzählen. I/he/she/it + was; you/we/they + were.

examples.en: At 9 o'clock I was at school.
examples.de: Um 9 Uhr war ich in der Schule.

examples.en: Peter and John were in their classroom.
examples.de: Peter und John waren in ihrem Klassenzimmer.

rules.en: Negative: wasn't (was not), weren't (were not).
rules.de: Verneinung: wasn't (was not), weren't (were not).

examples.de: Sandra und Kate waren nicht da.

rules.en: Questions: Was I/he/she/it …? Were you/we/they …? Short answers: Yes, I was. / No, they weren't.
rules.de: Fragen: Was I/he/she/it …? Were you/we/they …? Kurzantworten: Yes, I was. / No, they weren't.

examples.en: Was it cold? – Yes, it was.
Were you at the party? – No, I wasn't.
examples.de: Warst du auf der Party? – Nein.

commonErrors.description: Using were with I or was with they.
Using was with a plural subject.

structures.name: Time prepositions
structures.description: Using on (days/dates), in (months/seasons/parts of the day) and at (clock times) to talk about time.

rules.en: Use on for days and dates.
rules.de: Verwende on für Tage und Daten.

examples.de: Das Konzert ist am Donnerstag, dem 15. Juli.

rules.en: Use in for months and parts of the day.
rules.de: Verwende in für Monate und Tageszeiten.

examples.en: My sister's birthday is in December.
examples.de: Der Geburtstag meiner Schwester ist im Dezember.

examples.en: I have maths in the morning.

rules.en: Use at for clock times and for night.
rules.de: Verwende at für Uhrzeiten und für night.

examples.en: We go to bed late at night.
examples.de: Wir gehen spät in der Nacht ins Bett.

commonErrors.description: Using in instead of on with a day.
Using on instead of in with a month.

structures.name: Linking words (and, but, because)
structures.description: Joining ideas with and (addition), but (contrast) and because (reason). After because the word order is normal SVO.

rules.en: Use and to join two similar ideas and but to join two contrasting ideas.
rules.de: Verwende and, um zwei ähnliche Ideen zu verbinden, und but, um zwei gegensätzliche Ideen zu verbinden.

examples.en: The front of the spaceship opened and a big robot arm reached out.
examples.de: Die Vorderseite des Raumschiffs öffnete sich und ein großer Roboterarm streckte sich heraus.

examples.en: "Fly into the eye of the storm!" shouted Diana, but the pilot was not happy.
examples.de: „Flieg ins Auge des Sturms!", rief Diana, aber der Pilot war nicht glücklich.

rules.en: Use because to give a reason. After because the word order is normal (subject + verb + object).
rules.de: Verwende because, um einen Grund anzugeben. Nach because steht die normale Wortstellung (Subjekt + Verb + Objekt).

examples.en: Diana wanted to rescue the spaceship because her friends were on it.
examples.de: Diana wollte das Raumschiff retten, weil ihre Freunde an Bord waren.

examples.en: I like summer because it is warm.
examples.de: Ich mag den Sommer, weil es warm ist.

commonErrors.description: Using German verb-final word order after because.
commonErrors.wrong: I like summer because warm it is.
commonErrors.correct: I like summer because it is warm.

commonErrors.description: Adding of after because before a clause.
commonErrors.wrong: I'm happy because of I have a dog.
commonErrors.correct: I'm happy because I have a dog.

structures.name: Past simple (2) regular verbs
structures.description: Forming the past simple of regular verbs with -ed, plus spelling rules (-d, -ied, doubled consonant). Same form for all persons.

rules.en: For a regular verb, add -ed to the base form. The form is the same for all persons.
rules.de: Bei einem regelmäßigen Verb hängst du -ed an die Grundform. Die Form ist für alle Personen gleich.

examples.en: jump – jumped, play – played, help – helped
examples.de: jump – jumped, play – played, help – helped

rules.en: Spelling: verbs ending in -e add only -d (arrive → arrived); consonant + y changes to -ied (carry → carried); a short vowel + consonant doubles the consonant (stop → stopped).
rules.de: Schreibweise: Verben auf -e bekommen nur -d (arrive → arrived); Konsonant + y wird zu -ied (carry → carried); kurzer Vokal + Konsonant verdoppelt den Konsonanten (stop → stopped).

commonErrors.description: Forgetting to add -ed.
Forgetting to double the consonant.
commonErrors.wrong: The bus stoped at the corner.
commonErrors.correct: The bus stopped at the corner.

structures.name: Past simple (4–5) irregular verbs
structures.description: Common irregular verbs whose past simple is not formed with -ed and must be learnt by heart (have–had, go–went, see–saw …).

rules.en: Irregular verbs do NOT add -ed. They have special past forms you learn by heart.
rules.de: Unregelmäßige Verben bekommen KEIN -ed. Sie haben besondere Vergangenheitsformen, die du auswendig lernst.

examples.en: have – had, go – went, see – saw
examples.de: have – had, go – went, see – saw

examples.en: I went into the shop and took out the control.
examples.de: Ich ging in den Laden und holte die Fernbedienung heraus.

rules.en: More common irregular verbs: eat–ate, come–came, get–got, give–gave, tell–told, find–found, catch–caught, read–read.
rules.de: Weitere häufige unregelmäßige Verben: eat–ate, come–came, get–got, give–gave, tell–told, find–found, catch–caught, read–read.

examples.en: They ran out of the classroom.

commonErrors.description: Adding -ed to an irregular verb.
commonErrors.wrong: I goed to the cinema yesterday.
commonErrors.correct: I went to the cinema yesterday.

commonErrors.description: Regularising a vowel-change irregular verb.

structures.name: Past simple (3) negative with didn't
structures.description: Forming the past-simple negative with didn't + base verb. The form is the same for all persons.

rules.en: Make the past-simple negative with: person + didn't (did not) + base form of the verb (not the past form!). It is the same for all persons.
rules.de: Die Verneinung im Past simple bildest du so: Person + didn't (did not) + Grundform des Verbs (nicht die Vergangenheitsform!). Sie ist für alle Personen gleich.

examples.de: Ich habe das Buch nicht gelesen.
Der Film hat uns nicht gefallen.

commonErrors.description: Using the past form after didn't (double past marking — the classic error).
Keeping -ed on the verb after didn't.

structures.name: (be) going to
structures.description: Talking about future plans and intentions with the present of be + going to + base verb.

rules.en: To talk about plans for the future, use the present of be + going to + base verb.
rules.de: Wenn du über Pläne für die Zukunft sprichst, verwendest du das Present von be + going to + Grundform.

examples.en: They're going to visit their friends.
We're going to swim in the sea a lot.
examples.de: Wir werden viel im Meer schwimmen.

rules.en: Negative: present of be + not + going to + base verb.
rules.de: Verneinung: Present von be + not + going to + Grundform.

examples.en: We aren't going to stay at home.
examples.de: Wir werden nicht zu Hause bleiben.

rules.en: Questions: Am/Is/Are + subject + going to + base verb? Short answers: Yes, I am. / No, she isn't.
rules.de: Fragen: Am/Is/Are + Subjekt + going to + Grundform? Kurzantworten: Yes, I am. / No, she isn't.

examples.en: Are you going to lie in the sun?
examples.de: Wirst du in der Sonne liegen?

examples.en: What are you going to do in your holidays?
examples.de: Was wirst du in den Ferien machen?

commonErrors.description: Leaving out the be auxiliary before going to.
commonErrors.wrong: I going to play football tomorrow.
commonErrors.correct: I'm going to play football tomorrow.

commonErrors.description: Adding -ing to the main verb after going to.

sbRefs: g1/sb/SB Unit 15 What are you going to do?.txt#grammar-1

provenance.sbRef: g1/sb/SB Unit 15 What are you going to do?.txt#grammar-1

v1Waivers.note: Lexical set (the seven day names + capitalisation), not a MORE! 1 SB GRAMMAR box. The only grammatical content — on + day — is taught in the unit-12 Time prepositions box (structure prepositions-time).

Lexical/usage point (once/twice/three times a week, every + day). No dedicated SB GRAMMAR box: the unit-9 box covers question words, object pronouns and possessive 's. Distinct from the adverbs-of-frequency structure (always/usually/never), so not folded in.
